tedapi.description: Connect using the gateway's own Wi-Fi password (mode 4). Works on Powerwall 2, Powerwall+, and Powerwall 3 without enabling Customer Login.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the Powerwall gateway (e.g. 192.168.91.1)
data_description.gw_pwd: Full gateway Wi-Fi password printed on the QR label

hybrid.description: Combines a customer login with the gateway password to add supplemental vitals data. Requires Customer Login to be enabled on the gateway.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the Powerwall gateway
data_description.password: Customer password set up on the gateway
data_description.gw_pwd: Full gateway Wi-Fi password printed on the QR label

local.description: Connect using the gateway's Customer Login. Requires Customer Login to be enabled on the gateway first (via the Tesla app or gateway web UI).

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the Powerwall gateway
data_description.password: Customer password set up on the gateway

cloud.description: Uses Tesla's cloud API instead of a local connection. Requires a one-time login performed outside Home Assistant first: run `python -m pypowerwall setup` (or `setup -headless`) to create a `.pypowerwall.auth` file, then point this at the directory containing it.

data_description.authpath: Directory containing the .pypowerwall.auth file created by `python -m pypowerwall setup`
data_description.siteid: Tesla site ID to use if the account has more than one site

fleetapi.description: Uses Tesla's FleetAPI instead of a local connection. Requires a one-time setup performed outside Home Assistant first: register an app at developer.tesla.com, then run `python -m pypowerwall.fleetapi setup` to create a `.pypowerwall.fleetapi` file, and point this at the directory containing it.

data_description.authpath: Directory containing the .pypowerwall.fleetapi file created by `python -m pypowerwall.fleetapi setup`
data_description.siteid: Tesla site ID to use if the account has more than one site

tedapi_v1r.description: Full-protobuf LAN access for Powerwall 3, using a pre-registered RSA key. Requires a one-time setup performed outside Home Assistant first: run `python -m pypowerwall register` to generate and register an RSA-4096 key pair with the gateway (this may require briefly power-cycling the gateway to confirm), then point this at the resulting private key file.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the Powerwall gateway
data_description.gw_pwd: Full gateway Wi-Fi password printed on the QR label
data_description.rsa_key_path: Path to the RSA-4096 private key PEM file produced by `python -m pypowerwall register`
data_description.wifi_host: Alternate WiFi TEDAPI host to fall back to

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect. Check the connection details and try again.

abort.already_configured: This Powerwall gateway is already configured
